titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to photographs relating to European occupation, colonization, and conflicts in North and East Africa
Photographs Relating to European Occupation, Colonization, and Conflicts in North and East Africa
titlestmt.author: by Jessica Tai and Eve Neiger

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.unittitle: Photographs Relating to European Occupation, Colonization, and Conflicts in North and East Africa

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Primarily in Italian, with some materials in English, French, Portuguese, and Spanish.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Photographs Relating to European Occupation, Colonization, and Conflicts in North and East Africa is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Kevin Jackson on the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund, 2009-2018; on the Henry C. Taylor Fund, 2020.
May 2012 acquisition: Purchased from McBlain Books on the African General Fund, 2012.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Photographs Relating to European Occupation, Colonization, and Conflicts in North and East Africa. General Collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing and minimal organization.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection or revisions in arrangement and description.

Information included in the Content Description note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Portions of description for this collection have been directly repurposed from description supplied with the collection upon its receipt. Copies of the description received with the collection have been physically retained with the material. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into four series: I. East Africa, 1880 - 1955. II. North Africa, 1909 - 1962. III. Other and multiple locations, 1910-circa 1940s. IV. Conflicts, 1895 - 1956.

bioghist.head: History of colonization in Africa
bioghist.p: Beginning in the 1870s, Africa faced European imperialist aggression, diplomatic pressures, military invasions, and occupation and colonization. African rulers and peoples demonstrated various forms of resistance against the attempt to colonize their countries, including mobilizing local military to resist the seizure of their lands and the imposition of colonization. Despite these efforts, by 1900 much of Africa had been colonized by Britain, France, Germany, Belgium, Spain, Portugal, and Italy. Beginning in the 1950s, African countries began to gain independence from their colonizing nations. Although countries began to technically be considered autonomous nations with political independence, the many decades of colonial oppression and exploitation of African economic and cultural resources has left a damaging impact that continues to affect contemporary African society today.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This collection contains photograph albums, loose photographs, and postcards documenting the European colonization, occupation, and conflicts in North and East Africa. The collection documents a European colonial perspective of the colonization of African countries including Abyssinia (present day Ethiopia), Algeria, Eritrea, Kenya, Libya, Melilla, Morocco, and Somalia. Photographs depict African colonial subjects and other communities in North and East Africa, traditional housing, markets, and landscapes. Includes images showing interactions between colonizers and African colonial subjects, including askari soldiers and European colonial troops. Other images depict fascist monuments, colonial architecture, and infrastruture development including the building of roads, bridges, and railways to aid in the extractive colonial economy of exporting resources.

The collection documents conflicts including the Italo-Ethiopian War, 1895-1896, the Turco-Italian War, 1911-1912, World War I, 1914-1918, the Rif War, 1921-1926, the Italo-Ethiopian War, 1935-1936, and World War II, 1939-1945. Images depict Italian and Spanish colonial troops, askari, and African colonial subjects and civilians during European occupation, invasion, and colonization of African peoples and countries.
This collection contains harmful and racist depictions of African people, as well as images of atrocities including dead and mutilated bodies and African people who have been murdered by hanging.

did.unittitle: East Africa

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged into five subseries: Abyssinia, circa 1930s-1940s. Eritrea, circa 1880s-1947. Ethiopia, circa 1908-1949. Kenya, 1906-circa 1955. Somalia, 1897–1955.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains photographs, photograph albums, and postcards documenting African colonial subjects, European colonizers, Italian military, architecture, landscapes, and other scenes in locations including Abyssinia (present day Ethiopia), Eritrea, Ethiopia, Kenya, and Somalia.

did.unittitle: Abyssinia

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains photographs and postcards of Abyssinia (present day Ethiopia). Images include Abyssinian communities, landscapes, colonial architecture, traditional housing, road construction, Italian colonial soldiers, and askari soldiers.

did.unittitle: Eritrea

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains photographs, photograph albums, and postcards of Aden, Adi Keyh, Adi Ugri, Agordat, Asmara, Assab, Benadir, Barentu, Ghinda, Keren, Massawa, Nefasit, Saganeiti, Sanafe, and other locations in Eritrea. Images include African colonial subjects, Eritrean askari, Italian colonial soliders and colonizers, colonial architecture, traditional housing, road and railroad construction, markets, and coptic festivals.

did.unittitle: Ethiopia

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains photographs, photograph albums, and postcards documenting Adwa, Addis Ababa, Axum, Bishoftu, Dabat, Debre Tabor, Dessie, Dire Dawa, Lake Ashenge, and other locations in Ethiopia. Includes images of Ethiopian communities, Italian colonial troops, askari, colonial architecture, traditional housing, road construction, and the construction of fascist monuments.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains photographs, photograph albums, and postcards of Hafun, Kismayo, Merca, Mogadishu, and other locations in Somalia. Includes images of African colonial subjects, Italian colonial soldiers, colonial architecture, traditional housing, road and building construction, street views, landscapes, and propaganda photographs depicting infrastructure development.

did.unittitle: North Africa

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged into four subseries: Algeria, circa 1954-1962. Libya, circa 1910s- 1958. Melilla, 1909 - 1929. Morocco, circa 1920-1926.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains photographs, photograph albums, and postcards documenting African colonial subjects, European colonizers, French, Italian, and Turkish military, askari, architecture, landscapes, and other scenes in locations including Algeria, Libya, Melilla, and Morocco.

did.unittitle: Algeria

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains photographs, photograph albums, and postcards of Algeria. Images include documentation of the Algerian War of Independence and French soldiers and military life.

arrangement.head: Arrangement

acqinfo.p: Purchased from Kevin Jackson on the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund, 2011.

did.unittitle: Conflicts

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged into six subseries: Italo-Ethiopian War, 1895-1896, 1895. Turco-Italian War, 1911-1912, circa 1908-1914. World War I, 1914-1918, circa 1914-1918. Rif War, 1921-1926, 1907-1926. Italo-Ethiopian War, 1935-1936, 1933-1956. World War II, 1939-1945, 1935-1945.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains photographs, photograph albums, and postcards of conflicts including the Italo-Ethiopian War, 1895-1896, the Turco-Italian War, 1911-1912, World War I, 1914-1918, the Rif War, 1921-1926, the Italo-Ethiopian War, 1935-1936, and World War II, 1939-1945. Includes images of Italian and Spanish colonial troops, askari, and civilians during European occupation, invasion, and colonization of African peoples and countries.

did.unittitle: Italo-Ethiopian War, 1895-1896

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: The First Italo-Ethiopian War was fought between Italy and Ethiopia from 1895 to 1896. It originated from the disputed Treaty of Wuchale, which the Italians claimed turned Ethiopia into an Italian protectorate. Ethiopia's military victory over Italy secured it the distinction of being the only African territory to avoid absorption into a European colonial empire during the nineteenth century, although areas of what became Eritrea and of Somalia where at times Ethiopia had ruled did fall to Italy, becoming Italian East Africa.

did.unittitle: Card photograph of religious ceremony and troops in Massawa, Eritrea

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Kevin Jackson on the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund, 2012.

did.unittitle: Turco-Italian War, 1911-1912

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: The Turco-Italian War (1911–12) was undertaken by Italy to gain colonies in North Africa by invading the Turkish provinces of Tripolitana and Cyrenaica (modern day Libya). As a result of the war, Italy captured the Ottoman Tripolitania Vilayet, of which the main sub-provinces were Fezzan, Cyrenaica, and Tripoli. These territories became the colonies of Italian Tripolitania and Cyrenaica, which would later merge into Italian Libya.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains photographs and photograph albums documenting the Turco-Italian War, 1911-1912. Includes images of Italian colonial troops, Eritrean askari, and scenes of villages and people in Libya during the war.

did.unittitle: Italo-Ethiopian War, 1935-1936

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: The Italo-Ethiopian War, (1935–36), was an armed conflict that resulted in Ethiopia's subjection to Italian rule. Often seen as one of the episodes that paved the way for World War II, the war demonstrated the ineffectiveness of the League of Nations when League decisions were not supported by the great powers. The Italian government carried out a substantial number of war crimes in Ethiopia from 1935–1940. The most notable being the use of mustard gas and the bombing of a field hospital run by the Swedish Red Cross.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains photographs and photograph albums featuring images of Italian colonial troops, African colonial subjects in Eritrea, Abyssinian (Ethiopian) villages and communities, askari, and other scenes during the Italo-Ethiopian War.

did.unittitle: World War II, 1939-1945

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains photographs and photograph albums featuring images of the invasions by colonial troops of Ethiopia, Sudan, and Somalia, and African colonial subjects during World War II.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: World War II was a global war that lasted from 1939 to 1945. It involved the vast majority of the world's countries—including all of the great powers—forming two opposing military alliances: the Allies and the Axis powers. The North African campaign took place from 1940 to 1943 and included campaigns fought in the Libyan and Egyptian deserts, Morocco, Algeria and Tunisia. The campaign was fought between the Allies, many of whom had colonial interests in Africa dating from the late 19th century, and the Axis Powers. The East African campaign (also known as the Abyssinian campaign) was fought in East Africa by Allies of World War II, mainly from the British Empire, against Italy and its colony of Italian East Africa, between June 1940 and November 1941.
